sections.paragraph:
ὑστέρησις, εως, ἡ (ὑστερέω; Aq. Job 30:3; Aesop 106 P.=154 H./126 Ch./108 H-H.; Achmes 83, 13f; Cat. Cod. Astr. X 146, 20; 147, 2) the condition of lacking that which is essential, need, lack, poverty Mk 12:44. Pl. deprivations Hs 6, 3, 4.—καθʼ ὑστέρησιν because of need or want (κατά II 5aδ) Phil 4:11.—DELG s.v. ὕστερος. TW. Spicq.

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1044
